title: Use spawn + STDIN for untrusted content; cmd.exe routing on Windows
context: execFile() cannot launch Windows .cmd/.ps1 shims (only *.exe). On Windows, the npm `claude` is a shim, so the provider threw ENOENT, looked unavailable, and every synthesis fell back to low-confidence deterministic heuristic instead of using the subscription LLM.
decision: Replace execFile with spawn; on Windows route through cmd.exe (shell:true) to resolve .cmd shims; pass untrusted content (prompt/diff) via STDIN, never argv. This decouples shell-routing (needed for Windows) from content delivery (needed for safety): argv stays trusted and space-free, so shell interpretation can't reach user input.
consequences: CLI provider now available on Windows; synthesis uses subscription LLM, not fallback heuristic
Prompt/diff delivered via STDIN, isolated from shell metacharacter interpretation even though cmd.exe is involved
Avoids Node DEP0190 warning (untrusted args + shell:true); argv is only trusted, space-free flags
Explicit coupling between Windows shim resolution and STDIN safety design
alternatives_rejected: Keep execFile + patch Windows PATH: fragile, doesn't help STDIN safety
Use execFile + escape argv for shell: complex escaping logic, easy to miss edge cases
Spawn without shell on Windows: can't resolve .cmd shims
Concatenate command string: obvious injection vector, no isolation
